The CLIP text encoder has a critical bug where it does not properly apply attention masks and causal masking, leading to incorrect output results. The issues are:

Missing causal masking: CLIP's original design uses causal attention, but the current implementation has causal=False, causin

sglang.title: Add fused FP8 KV cache write kernel for TRTLLM MHA backend
sglang.author: harvenstar
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
This PR introduces a fused FP8 KV cache write kernel for the TRTLLM MHA backend.
It combines FP8 quantization and paged KV cache writes into a single Triton kernel (plus a naive fallback) to reduce kernel launch overhead and avoid intermediate FP8 tensors.

Motivation
The current FP8 KV cach

sglang.title: fix rmsnorm -> layernorm in qwen3 omni
sglang.author: vincentzed
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
If you look at the transformer's modeling code, then you will notice that it's actually supposed to be layer norm instead of RMS norm.
You can transform RMS norm to be the same as layer norm with a subtraction, but we don't have that offset available in SGL.

vllm.title: [ROCm][MXFP4] Infer w4a4 quant method in rocm aiter fused moe
vllm.author: ZhiweiYan-96
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
The quant recipe that models use is with bf16/fp16 activation dynamic quantized + fp4 weight static quantized (aka w4a16). This PR make this recipe as the default path and we successfully verify it on DeepSeek-R1 with MXFP4, while the code w/o this modification would trigger quant kernel jit

vllm.author: maxyanghu

This PR adds an optional server arg --enable-layerwise-nvtx-tracing. When enabled it uses forward hooks to trace detailed Pytorch module information such as input/output shapes, module name etc. This is especially useful when the users want to correspond CUDA kernels with Pytorch modules/mod

vllm.author: hmellor

I feel like I've been explaining how to use pre-commit a lot lately. The instructions are always the same.
This PR improves the pre-commit GitHub Actions workflow so that if pre-commit fails (for a non-draft PR) it'll leave a comment explaining how to install pre-commit.

When prefill and decode instances have incompatible versions or configurations, it is possible that the handshake will fail with unclear errors, NIXL transfers will fail, or even server crashes might occur. This change adds explicit compatibility checks during handshake to

vllm.author: NickLucche
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Pretty straightforward PR:

xfer_time_s is not used as we're grabbing time directly from nixl
self.device_id is being assigned 3 times, with the last one overriding previous assignments

vllm.title: [BugFix] Eagerly abort cancelled final-step requests
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Currently, when requests are cancelled while executing their final step, "completion" of those requests is subsequently handled based on normal stop processing (e.g. length or stop token), and so the abort essentially has no effect.
This is typically not a problem since the final output would be ign

vllm.title: [NIXL] Add remote_request_id to kv_transfer_params
vllm.author: markmc
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Include the internal request ID that the prefill instance is expecting the decode instance to send it in the NIXL notification.
Right now, we rely on the proxy supplying the ID via X-Request-ID and that prefill and decode will mangle this ID in identical ways. This is obviously quite brittle, and P

vllm.title: [Attention][UX][1/N] Add AttentionConfig and change attention env vars to CLI arguments
vllm.author: MatthewBonanni
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
As part of the effort to reduce env variables (#25700), this PR introduces AttentionConfig and the --attention-config CLI argument group. The following environment variables are converted to CLI arguments:
VLLM_ATTENTION_BACKEND                        -> --attention-config.backend
VLLM_FLASH

vllm.title: [Compile] Conditional compilation. Introduce compile_ranges
vllm.author: ilmarkov
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Second part of splitting #22086
Dynamic Graph dispatch via compile_ranges: Introduces a new configuration option, compile_ranges, as an alternative to compile_sizes. This enables dynamic dispatch to different compiled graphs based on the input batch size.
Now with this approach, when allreduce fusio

vllm.title: [KVConnector][Feature] Support KV connector cache reset via /reset_prefix_cache
vllm.author: ptovam
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Extends the existing /reset_prefix_cache endpoint with an optional reset_external flag.
When set, both the local (GPU) and external (connector) prefix caches are reset.
This allows clearing connector-side caches dynamically without restarting the server -useful for benchmarking and testing.

vllm.title: [Enc-Dec] Fix OOT tokenizer issue
vllm.author: NickLucche
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Generalizing Whisper logic yet again to all enc-dec models, so that OOT ones can be supported.
In this case, we need to ensure tokenizer does not add special chars to decoder prompt.

vllm.title: Better error when world size is larger than node and `distributed_executor_backend` is not set

#25775 removed the automatic fallback to Ray to prevent non-Ray users from hitting a 30 minute timeout if they accidentally set the world size (tp * pp * sp) to be greater than the number of GPUs in their node.
Unfortunately, it did not adequately explain what to do if you were a Ray user.
This PR i

vllm.title: [CI/Build][AMD] Skip marlin, machete, and hadacore tests since these require _C functions not defined for ROCm
vllm.author: rasmith
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR skips the marlin, machete, and hadacore tests since these require _C functions that are not defined for ROCm.  The functions are marlin_int4_fp8_preprocess, gptq_marlin_24_gemm, gptq_marlin_gemm , machete_prepack_B, and 'hadacore_transform'.

vllm.title: Fix AWQ MoE marlin check issue in marlin_utils.py for AMD backend
vllm.author: yuttian1
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
This PR disables Marlin support for AWQ dense and MoE layers when running on ROCm, preventing the runtime from calling the unavailable awq_marlin_repack operator.
Problem
On ROCm builds, the vllm._C extension does not include the awq_marlin_repack operator.
However, both check_marlin_support

vllm.title: [Model] Add support for transformer-based Ultravox v0.7 projector
vllm.author: petersalas
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Add support for transformer-based Ultravox adapters, such as those used in Ultravox v0.7.
Test Plan
Tested speech understanding on Ultravox models with the linear projector and the transformer-based projector.
Test Result
Both models work correctly.

vllm.title: [Bugfix]: Fix `TokenizerLike` interface
vllm.author: Rohan138
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Corrected+rebased version of #22121 to fix #22013. Note that this function is only called from RandomDataset.sample, which calls tokenizer.encode(prompt, add_special_tokens=False) (https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/main/vllm/benchmarks/datasets.py#L407). Hence, the tokenizer only add

vllm.title: [CI/Build][AMD] Use ROCM_ATTN instead of FLASH_ATTN test for test_register_kv_caches for ROCm and update test for TRITON_ATTN
vllm.author: rasmith
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This test skips FLASH_ATTN  for test_register_kv_caches since it is not supported on ROCm.
This also  updates test_register_kv_caches for TRITON_ATTN which was failing with the following error:
            # Verify get_reg_descs was called with caches_data
            assert mock_wrapper_instance.ge

vllm.title: [Misc] Move `disable_nccl_for_dp_synchronization` init logic into `VllmConfig`
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Follow-on from #29311, I realized that this check / implicit update should be done in VllmConfig.__post_init__ for consistency with similar logic and so that it runs after implicit async scheduler enabling/disabling is done.
This also means it will behave as intended if initializing with a VllmConfi

vllm.title: [Model Runner V2] Support min-p sampling
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR implements min-p sampling. Unlike the existing implementation in V1, this PR avoids softmax by directly comparing logits.

vllm.title: [bugfix] fix type[AttentionBackend] bug in kv_connector_base_v1
vllm.author: HF-001
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
fix the bug in KVConnectorBase_v1, change type[AttentionBackend] to  type["AttentionBackend"]

vllm.title: prefix caching design doc sha256 now default
vllm.author: redwrasse
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Updates prefix caching design doc comment to indicate sha256 is now the default hash function since v0.10.2.

vllm.title: [Chore] Deprecate `SupportsMultiModal.merge_by_field_config`
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Follow-up to #30035
Since merge_by_field_config is no longer effective, we can remove it from all model classes early and emit errors/warning messages for OOT models that still define merge_by_field_config to help them migrate.
Test Plan
Test Result
